highlights: OpenAI has launched GPT-5.6 with multi-tier options and crucial programmatic tool calling, while Meta released Muse Spark 1.1, a multimodal model geared for complex agentic tasks on their new API.

Significant advancements in AI agent efficiency are emerging, with new research demonstrating how optimized orchestration can drastically cut token costs and how agents can self-evolve by creating higher-order tools.

Industry adoption accelerates, highlighted by GPT-5.6's integration into Microsoft 365 Copilot, Google's enhanced managed agent capabilities for developers, and Google Research's new health-focused foundation model, SensorFM.

agents.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
agents.summary: OpenAI has launched 'ChatGPT Work', a new agent capable of taking action across multiple applications and files, persisting on projects for extended periods, and transforming high-level goals into completed tasks.

agents.title: From Atomic Actions to Standard Operating Procedures: Iterative Tool Optimization for Self-Evolving LLM Agents
agents.summary: EvoSOP, a new framework, empowers LLM agents to achieve self-evolution by synthesizing granular atomic actions into reusable Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), which significantly boosts task success rates and reduces interaction rounds.

agents.title: The Harness Effect: How Orchestration Design Sets the Token Economics of Enterprise Agentic AI
agents.summary: New research reveals that the orchestration layer (harness) is critical for enterprise agentic AI, demonstrating that effective design can cut blended cost per task by 41% and median wall-clock time by 44% while maintaining quality.

models.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna): A Three-Tier Model Family With Programmatic Tool Calling in the Responses API
models.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.6 in three tiers (Sol, Terra, Luna), featuring a significant developer advancement with Programmatic Tool Calling, enabling models to orchestrate tools by executing JavaScript in an isolated V8 runtime, drastically cutting token usage.

models.title: Google Research Introduces SensorFM: A Wearable Health Foundation Model Pretrained on One Trillion Minutes of Sensor Data
models.summary: Google Research, DeepMind, and collaborators unveiled SensorFM, a wearable health foundation model pretrained on over a trillion minutes of unlabeled sensor data from 5,000,000 participants, demonstrating superior performance on 34 of 35 tasks over feature-engineered baselines.

models.title: Meta Superintelligence Labs Releases Muse Spark 1.1: A Multimodal Reasoning Model for Agentic Tasks on Meta Model API
models.summary: Meta Superintelligence Labs launched Muse Spark 1.1, a multimodal reasoning model designed for agentic tasks, featuring a 1,000,000-token context window and zero-shot generalization to new tools, available via a public preview of the Meta Model API.

tools.title: Google AI Studio Adds Import from GitHub to Build a Deployable App
tools.summary: Google AI Studio now supports importing GitHub repositories directly into Build mode, allowing developers to transform existing code into a runtime-compatible format for iteration and deployment, streamlining the application development workflow.

tools.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API: background tasks, remote MCP and more
tools.summary: Google is enhancing Managed Agents in the Gemini API with new capabilities like background tasks and remote Multi-Agent Coordination Protocol (MCP), enabling developers to build more reliable and production-ready AI agents.

tools.title: Native-speed vLLM transformers modeling backend
tools.summary: Hugging Face has introduced a native-speed vLLM transformers modeling backend, significantly optimizing the inference performance for large language models, allowing developers to serve LLMs with greater efficiency.

companies.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
companies.summary: OpenAI announced that GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model powering Microsoft 365 Copilot, delivering stronger AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork to enable faster, higher-quality work for enterprise users.

companies.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
companies.summary: Deutsche Telekom is transforming into an AI-native telco by leveraging OpenAI's technology to revolutionize customer service, enhance employee workflows, optimize network operations, and innovate the future of voice communications.

companies.title: Netflix AI Team Cuts Wide-Partition Read Latency from Seconds to Milliseconds by Splitting Cassandra Partitions Per ID
companies.summary: Netflix engineers have dramatically reduced wide-partition read latency in Apache Cassandra from seconds to milliseconds by implementing dynamic partitioning, which detects and splits oversized partitions per TimeSeries ID on the read path.

learning.title: Helping K–12 educators build practical AI skills
learning.summary: OpenAI Academy, in partnership with the Walton Family Foundation, is launching 'AI Skills Jams' to provide K–12 educators with hands-on training to build practical AI skills for effective classroom integration.

learning.title: Ask an AI expert: What exactly is the full stack?
learning.summary: A Google expert provides a clear explanation of what it means to adopt a 'full-stack' approach to AI development, outlining why this comprehensive methodology is fundamental to Google's long-standing AI work.

learning.title: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 3): Attention is all you profile
learning.summary: This technical guide, the third part of a series on PyTorch profiling, focuses specifically on optimizing attention mechanisms within models, offering practical insights for developers to enhance model performance and efficiency.
